member.name: T:Salem.Drawing.BasicDirections
member.summary: Specifies the four basic cardinal directions: up, down, left, and right.
member.remarks: This enumeration is typically used to represent movement or orientation in two-dimensional space, such as in user interfaces, or navigation logic. The underlying type is byte.

member.name: T:Salem.Drawing.ArrowStyles
member.summary: Specifies the available styles for rendering arrow shapes in graphical components.
member.remarks: Use this enumeration to select the visual appearance of arrows, such as chevrons, triangles, or lines, when drawing or displaying directional indicators. The specific style chosen affects the thickness, fill, and overall look of the arrow. The meaning of each style value depends on the context in which it is used.

member.name: T:Salem.Drawing.ColorModes
member.summary: Specifies the available visual themes for the application user interface.
member.remarks: Use this enumeration to select between predefined appearance modes, such as light or dark themes. The selected theme may affect colors, backgrounds, and overall UI styling throughout the application.

member.name: T:Salem.Drawing.RenderModes
member.summary: Specifies the visual rendering style for user interface elements.
member.remarks: Use this enumeration to select the appearance mode for controls or components that support multiple visual styles. The available modes correspond to common UI themes, such as the system default, professional, manager, or Office Flat styles. The effect of each mode depends on the control or component using this enumeration.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.FillRect(System.Drawing.Graphics,System.Drawing.Rectangle,System.Drawing.Color)
member.summary: Fills the specified rectangle on the provided graphics surface with the given color.
member.param: The graphics surface on which to fill the rectangle. Cannot be null.
The rectangle to fill, specified in the coordinate space of the graphics surface.
The color to use when filling the rectangle.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.DrawSimpleBorder(System.Drawing.Graphics,System.Drawing.Rectangle,System.Drawing.Color,System.Single)
member.summary: Draws a simple rectangular border on the specified graphics surface using the given color and line width.
member.remarks: The border is drawn inside the bounds of the specified rectangle. This method does not fill the rectangle; it only draws the outline.
member.param: The graphics surface on which to draw the border. Cannot be null.
The rectangle that defines the bounds of the border to draw.
The color of the border.
The width, in pixels, of the border line. Must be greater than 0.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.OffsetRect(System.Drawing.Rectangle,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a new Rectangle that is offset by the specified horizontal and vertical amounts.
member.param: The Rectangle to offset.
The amount, in pixels, to offset the Rectangle horizontally. Positive values move the Rectangle to the right; negative values move it to the left.
The amount, in pixels, to offset the Rectangle vertically. Positive values move the Rectangle downward; negative values move it upward.
member.returns: A Rectangle that is the result of offsetting the input Rectangle by the specified amounts.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.OffsetRect(System.Drawing.Rectangle,System.Drawing.Point)
member.summary: Returns a new Rectangle that is offset from the specified rectangle by the coordinates of the specified point.
member.param: The Rectangle to offset.
A Point whose X and Y values specify the horizontal and vertical distances to offset the rectangle.
member.returns: A Rectangle that is the result of offsetting the original rectangle by the specified point.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.TranslateOrigin(System.Drawing.Rectangle)
member.summary: Returns a new rectangle with the same size as the specified rectangle, but with its origin translated to (0, 0).
member.param: The rectangle to translate so that its top-left corner is at the origin.
member.returns: A rectangle with the same width and height as , but with its X and Y coordinates set to 0.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.ApplyCustomShape(System.Windows.Forms.Control,System.Drawing.Graphics,Salem.Drawing.ShapeInfo)
member.summary: Applies a custom shape to the specified control and renders it using the provided graphics context.
member.remarks: This method both draws the custom shape onto the control and updates the control's region to match the specified shape. Use this method to visually and interactively apply non-rectangular shapes to controls.
member.param: The control to which the custom shape will be applied. Cannot be null.
The graphics context used to render the custom shape. Cannot be null.
The shape information that defines the appearance and region of the custom shape. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.DrawCustomShape(System.Windows.Forms.Control,System.Drawing.Graphics,Salem.Drawing.ShapeInfo,System.Int16)
member.summary: Draws a custom-shaped border onto the specified control using the provided graphics context and shape information.
member.remarks: If the control has a parent, the background is cleared using the parent's background color; otherwise, black is used. The method supports both rectangles and rectangles with individually rounded corners, as specified by the shape information.
member.param: The control on which the custom shape is to be drawn. The control's background color is used to fill the shape.
The graphics context used to render the shape. This must be valid and associated with the target control.
An object containing details about the shape to draw, including border size, border color, dash style, and corner radii.
The number of pixels by which to shrink the shape from each edge. Must be greater than or equal to 0. The default is 0.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.ApplyCustomRegion(System.Windows.Forms.Control,Salem.Drawing.ShapeInfo)
member.summary: Applies a custom region to the specified control based on the provided shape information, enabling rounded corners or other non-rectangular shapes.
member.remarks: This method modifies the control's Region property to match the specified shape. If all corner radii are zero, the region will be set to the control's rectangular bounds. Otherwise, the region will reflect the custom shape defined by the corner radii. Changing the region may affect hit testing and rendering of the control.
member.param: The control to which the custom region will be applied. Cannot be null.
An object containing the corner radius values and shape details used to define the region. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.GetArrowChar(Salem.Drawing.BasicDirections,Salem.Drawing.ArrowStyles)
member.summary: Returns the Unicode character representing an arrow for the specified direction and style in the "Segoe MDL2 Assets", "Segoe Fluent Icons" fonts.
member.param: The direction for which to retrieve the arrow character.
The visual style of the arrow to use.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.MapToBasicDirections(System.Windows.Forms.ArrowDirection)
member.summary: Maps an ArrowDirection value to its corresponding BasicDirections value.
member.param: The ArrowDirection value to convert.
member.exception: Thrown when the direction is not a valid ArrowDirection value.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.DrawingHelpers.GetSystemTheme
member.summary: Retrieves the current system color mode preference for applications.
member.returns: The current color mode as specified by the system theme setting.

member.name: T:Salem.Drawing.HslColor
member.summary: Represents a color in the HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) color space with an alpha (transparency) component.
member.remarks: The HSL color model describes colors in terms of hue, saturation, and lightness, which can be more intuitive for certain color manipulations compared to the RGB model. The HslColor struct provides methods for creating HSL colors from ARGB or Color values and for converting HSL values back to standard Color instances. All component values are expected to be within their standard ranges: hue in degrees (typically 0–360), saturation and lightness as fractions (0–1), and alpha as a byte (0–255).

member.name: P:Salem.Drawing.HslColor.Alpha
member.summary: Gets or sets the alpha (transparency) component of the color.

member.name: P:Salem.Drawing.HslColor.Hue
member.summary: Gets or sets the hue component of the color.

member.name: P:Salem.Drawing.HslColor.Saturation
member.summary: Gets or sets the saturation component of the color.

member.name: P:Salem.Drawing.HslColor.Lightness
member.summary: Gets or sets the lightness component of the color.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.HslColor.FromAhsl(System.Byte,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a new HSL color with the specified alpha, hue, saturation, and lightness components.
member.param: The alpha component of the color. Represents opacity, where 0 is fully transparent and 255 is fully opaque.
The hue component of the color, in degrees. Typically ranges from 0 to 360.
The saturation component of the color, as a value between 0 and 1, where 0 is fully desaturated and 1 is fully saturated.
The lightness component of the color, as a value between 0 and 1, where 0 is black and 1 is white.
member.returns: A new HslColor instance with the specified alpha, hue, saturation, and lightness values.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.HslColor.FromArgb(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Creates a new HSL color from the specified ARGB (alpha, red, green, blue) values.
member.remarks: The resulting HSL color is computed from the provided ARGB values using standard color conversion. The alpha value is preserved in the resulting HslColor.
member.param: The alpha component of the color. Specifies the opacity, where 0 is fully transparent and 255 is fully opaque.
The red component of the color. Valid values are 0 through 255.
The green component of the color. Valid values are 0 through 255.
The blue component of the color. Valid values are 0 through 255.
member.returns: An HslColor instance representing the color defined by the specified ARGB values.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.HslColor.FromColor(System.Drawing.Color)
member.summary: Creates a new HslColor instance from the specified ARGB color.
member.param: The Color to convert to an HSL representation.
member.returns: An HslColor instance that represents the equivalent hue, saturation, and lightness values of the specified color.

member.name: M:Salem.Drawing.HslColor.ToColor(System.Byte,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a color from the specified alpha, hue, saturation, and lightness (HSL) values.
member.remarks: If the saturation is 0, the resulting color is a shade of gray determined by the lightness value. The method clamps all calculated color channel values to the valid byte range (0 to 255).
member.param: The alpha component of the color. Valid values are 0 (fully transparent) to 255 (fully opaque).
The hue of the color, in degrees. Valid values are from 0 to 360, where 0 and 360 represent red, 120 represents green, and 240 represents blue.
The saturation of the color, as a value between 0 and 1. A value of 0 produces a shade of gray; 1 produces the most vivid color.
The lightness of the color, as a value between 0 and 1. A value of 0 produces black; 1 produces white.
member.returns: A Color structure representing the color defined by the specified alpha, hue, saturation, and lightness values.

member.name: T:Salem.Drawing.ShapeInfo
member.summary: Represents the border and corner radius settings for a rectangular shape.
member.remarks: Use this structure to specify the appearance of a shape's border, including the size and style of each corner radius, border thickness, color, and dash style. All values are expressed in device-independent units where applicable.
